title: Roblox Q1 2023 Earnings Results
text: Roblox released its first-quarter 2023 financial results on May 10, 2023, delivering mixed performance on key metrics. The company exceeded Wall Street expectations on bookings, posting $773.8 million compared to the anticipated $766 million. This stronger-than-expected revenue helped drive positive stock trading that day. However, Roblox fell short on earnings-per-share (EPS), posting a loss of $0.44 per share versus the projected loss of $0.40, representing a notable underperformance on profitability measures. The platform demonstrated robust growth in user activity. Hours engaged reached 14.5 billion, up 23 percent year-over-year, while Average Daily Active Users (DAUs) climbed to 66.1 million, representing a 22 percent increase from Q1 2022. The earnings announcement coincided with Roblox's decision to cease monthly key metrics reporting following a March 2023 shortfall. Despite the EPS miss, investor sentiment remained positive, likely reflecting confidence in the company's core bookings growth and user engagement trajectory.
